Author: Qi Zheng <zhengqi.arch@bytedance.com>
mm: memcontrol: correct the nr_pages parameter type of mem_cgroup_update_lru_size()

This commit updates the nr_pages parameter of mem_cgroup_update_lru_size()
from int to long.

> diff --git a/mm/memcontrol.c b/mm/memcontrol.c
> index 4a78550f6174..6491ca74b339 100644
> --- a/mm/memcontrol.c
> +++ b/mm/memcontrol.c
> @@ -1466,7 +1466,7 @@ struct lruvec *folio_lruvec_lock_irqsave(struct folio *folio,
>   * to or just after a page is removed from an lru list.
>   */
>  void mem_cgroup_update_lru_size(struct lruvec *lruvec, enum lru_list lru,
> -				int zid, int nr_pages)
> +				int zid, long nr_pages)
>  {
>  	struct mem_cgroup_per_node *mz;
>  	unsigned long *lru_size;

Since nr_pages is now a long, should the WARN_ONCE format string later in
this function be updated to use %ld?

Looking at the function body:

	if (WARN_ONCE(size < 0,
		"%s(%p, %d, %d): lru_size %ld\n",
		__func__, lruvec, lru, nr_pages, size)) {
		VM_BUG_ON(1);
		*lru_size = 0;
	}

Currently, it still uses %d for nr_pages. Could this cause a format string
mismatch warning on 64-bit architectures and result in the truncation of the
logged value?
